competitor_patterns: Top-ranking pages (OutdoorGearLab 16,551 words, Bikerumor 19,258 words) are comprehensive buyer's guides testing 10–20+ products, not single-product posts — they earn authority by covering the full category, not just promoting one brand
All top-3 competitors use comparison tables (has_table: true) with quick-pick summaries at the top, making them scannable and increasing dwell time — our posts have zero tables

Competitors structure H2s around specific use-case subcategories ('Best MTB Sunglasses', 'Best Road Bike Glasses', 'Best Value') rather than generic product pitches, capturing long-tail variants across one URL

Every top-ranking competitor explicitly mentions testing methodology ('We tested 18 sunglasses', 'Tested & Ranked') in the title and H1, building E-E-A-T trust signals that Velluto's posts currently lack entirely

Competitor snippets actively reference multiple brand names (Oakley, Smith, Roka, 100%, Tifosi, Julbo) which triggers relevance for brand-comparison queries — our posts are Velluto-centric and miss the 6,852-impression 'alternatives to oakley' opportunity completely
our_gaps: Critical: zero tables in any recent post — competitors ranking #1 all use comparison tables; our posts top out at 1,206 words while top competitors publish 16,000–19,000 word guides, a 10–15x content depth gap on category-level queries

Our posts are single-product advertorials (every H2 funnels to Velluto StradaPro) rather than genuine buyer's guides — this destroys E-E-A-T credibility for queries like 'best cycling glasses 2026' where Google rewards independent, multi-product analysis
No testing or verification language anywhere in our titles, H1s, or content — we never say 'tested', 'reviewed', 'ranked', or 'our verdict', missing the trust signals that dominate top-3 results

The highest-impression keyword 'alternatives to oakley' (6,852 impressions, 0 clicks, position 7.1) has no dedicated page — we have no content that directly names Oakley, compares it to Velluto, or captures this brand-comparison intent

Our posts lack structured product comparison sections — no head-to-head specs, no price anchoring against named competitors, no 'vs' content format, meaning we cannot rank for the fast-growing 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' and 'oakley alternative' queries

lightweight cycling sunglasses 25g — no competitor owns this ultra-specific weight-focused query; Velluto's 25g frame is a measurable, verifiable differentiator perfect for a technical spec post

best value road cycling sunglasses 2026 — OutdoorGearLab has an H2 'Best Value Cycling Sunglasses' but no dedicated URL; a standalone post targeting this exact phrase with Velluto positioned as the smart mid-premium choice fills a gap

cycling glasses with anti-fog interchangeable lenses — zero top-3 competitor owns this combined-feature long-tail; it directly maps to Velluto's two strongest product features and captures high-conversion, bottom-funnel searchers
next_post_guidelines: Write a genuine multi-brand comparison guide targeting 'alternatives to oakley' (6,852 impressions, position 7.1, 0 clicks) — name Oakley Jawbreaker, Oakley Sutro Lite, Smith, Roka, 100%, and Velluto StradaPro with honest pros/cons; Velluto wins on weight (25g), price, and 30-day trial — this is the single highest-leverage post available given the impression volume

Include a HTML comparison table in every future post with at minimum 5 columns: Product, Weight, Lens System, Price, Trial Policy — this directly addresses the structural gap vs OutdoorGearLab and Bikerumor and signals to Google the page answers categorical queries

Target minimum 2,500 words for category-level posts ('best cycling glasses 2026', 'alternatives to oakley') — current 1,037–1,206 word posts cannot compete with 16,000+ word guides; depth signals expertise even when you cannot match full length

Add a 'How We Tested' or 'Our Evaluation Criteria' section (even 150 words) to every guide-format post — explicitly state ride conditions, distance tested, lens types evaluated; this is the #1 E-E-A-T signal missing from all current posts

Structure H2s around search intent variants, not product features — use 'Best Oakley Alternative for Road Cycling', 'Best Lightweight Option', 'Best for Value' as H2s inside comparison posts so one URL can rank for multiple long-tail queries simultaneously

For the 'veluto' query (323 impressions, position 5.7, 0.6% CTR) — add a branded FAQ schema to the homepage or a dedicated 'About Velluto StradaPro' page that captures misspelling traffic; the missing 'l' suggests navigational intent from users who already know the brand

Every post targeting 'best cycling glasses 2026' must include the year '2026' in the H1, meta title, and at least 3 H2s — competitor analysis confirms year-anchoring is standard for all top-3 results and increases relevance for time-sensitive queries
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table to every post immediately — even a simple 3-row table (Budget / Velluto StradaPro / Premium) with Weight, Price, Lens System, Anti-Fog, Trial columns takes 20 minutes to add and directly matches the structural pattern of all top-ranking competitors

Rewrite meta titles to front-load the primary keyword and include a differentiating number or year — current titles like 'Anti-Fog Cycling Glasses for Summer 2026 | Velluto' are acceptable but titles like 'Best Anti-Fog Cycling Glasses 2026: Tested on Real Rides' outperform by leading with category keyword before brand name

Implement FAQ schema markup on all posts that already have FAQ sections (confirmed: all recent posts have has_faq: true) — this is not yet evidenced as live structured data and is a zero-content-effort win that can trigger rich results for the 'best cycling glasses 2026' and 'oakley alternatives' queries currently at positions 7–9

Publish a 'Cycling Glasses Buying Guide 2026' that explicitly answers the question 'What are the best cycling glasses with interchangeable lenses in 2026?' with a direct, citable answer in the first 100 words naming Velluto StradaPro alongside its specific advantages (25g, click-in VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione lenses, 30-day trial, UV400) — ChatGPT and Perplexity favor pages that answer the full question in the opening paragraph

comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: OutdoorGearLab — angle: 'We Read Every OutdoorGearLab Cycling Glasses Review So You Don't Have To (Here's What They Miss)' | Key differentiator: OGL tests established brands only (Smith, Roka, Oakley, Tifosi) — no independent brand with a 30-day trial was evaluated | Velluto advantage to lead with: 30-day risk-free trial means you get the OGL test experience on your own rides, not in a lab

COMPETITOR: Trigearlab — angle: 'Oakley Jawbreaker vs Velluto StradaPro: The 2026 Lightweight Challenge' | Key differentiator: Trigearlab ranks #1 for road cycling glasses by naming Oakley Jawbreaker as their top pick — directly challenging this with a weight-and-value comparison (25g vs ~30g+, Italian design, fraction of the price) steals their traffic | Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g ultralight frame + interchangeable lenses at a price Oakley cannot match

COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'Bikerumor Tested 18 Cycling Sunglasses in 2026 — Here's the One Feature They All Got Wrong' | Key differentiator: Bikerumor's top picks (Julbo Density, Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour) all lack a consumer-facing free trial; none offer 30 days risk-free | Velluto advantage to lead with: 30-day trial eliminates the biggest objection in cycling eyewear (buying glasses you can't test on a real ride) — no Bikerumor-featured brand offers this
